description: Positions one vector so its tail starts at another vector's tip, implementing tail-to-tip graphical vector addition.
utility: Position a vector's tail at another vector's tip for tail-to-tip addition

arguments.name: vecA
arguments.description: The target vector (chain attaches to its tip)

arguments.name: vecB
arguments.description: The source vector (moved to start at vecA's tip)

alternates.description: Place vecB's tail at vecA's tip

examples.scenario: Chain a vertical vector to a horizontal one (vector addition)
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
A = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 0))
B = vector(G, O, point(G, 0, 2))
C = chain(G, A, B)

O = point(G, 0, 0)
V1 = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 0))
V2 = vector(G, O, point(G, 1, 2))
V3 = vector(G, O, point(G, -1, 1))
C1 = chain(G, V1, V2)
C2 = chain(G, C1, V3)

O = point(G, 0, 0)
A = vector(G, O, point(G, 4, 1))
B = vector(G, O, point(G, 1, 3))
chained = chain(G, A, B)
resultant = vector(G, O, point(G, 5, 4), c(red))

examples.scenario: Chain two force vectors for equilibrium analysis
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
F1 = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 4))
F2 = vector(G, O, point(G, -2, 1))
F_sum = chain(G, F1, F2)
